scope: Two narrowly-scoped fixes in runtime/src/tui/components/App.tsx: (1) replace hard-coded conversationId={"agenc"} with the real id from props.session; (2) when there is no initial prompt but startup messages exist, enqueue them and trigger the turn through the session API instead of calling the local React submit(""), which short-circuits and leaves the messages stranded in the idle queue.

approvedExceptions.reason: The literal token 'unknown' in the scanned target files is the TypeScript `unknown` type keyword (used for type-safe casts and bridge interface fields), not shortcut/uncertainty language.

requiredBehaviors: OpenClaudeBridgeSession exposes readonly conversationId: string so the TUI can read the live session id (matches Session.conversationId in runtime/src/session/session.ts:688)
App.tsx Messages prop conversationId is sourced from props.session.conversationId, not the literal string 'agenc'
When two distinct sessions are mounted, the conversationId prop reflects each session's real id (no shared/static fallback in the live render path)
commands: node -e "const s=require('fs').readFileSync('runtime/src/tui/components/App.tsx','utf8'); if (/conversationId=\\{\"agenc\"\\}/.test(s)) { console.error('hard-coded conversationId still present'); process.exit(1); } if (!/conversationId=\\{props\\.session\\.conversationId\\}/.test(s)) { console.error('conversationId not sourced from props.session.conversationId'); process.exit(1); } console.log('ok'); "

requiredBehaviors: When initialPrompt is empty AND initialUserMessages.length > 0, useInitialSubmit must NOT call the local React submit("") (which short-circuits on empty text and leaves the idle queue undrained)
Each startup message is enqueued via session.enqueueIdleInput before the turn is triggered
After enqueue, the turn is triggered through the session API (session.submit("", { displayUserMessage: null })), matching the canonical pattern in runtime/src/agents/thread-manager.ts:535
When initialPrompt is non-empty, behavior is preserved: enqueue startup messages then call the local submit(initialPrompt)
useInitialSubmit fires at most once per mount (submitted ref preserved)
